name: Traditional Hamantaschen
recipeInstructions:
This recipe, adapted from The Jewish Festival Cookbook (p. 110), offers a yeasted version of Hamantaschen that is popular in Israel. Dissolve the yeast in the lukewarm milk, ensuring it's not too warm. Stir in 1 tablespoon sugar and set aside.
In a large bowl, combine butter, remaining sugar, salt, and scalded milk. Mix until well combined. When lukewarm, stir in the yeast mixture. Add beaten eggs and about 2 cups flour, beating to a smooth batter. Gradually add the remaining flour for a tender dough. Knead on a floured surface for about 2 minutes.
Grease a large bowl, then grease the entire dough ball. Place in the bowl and cover. Let rise in a warm place until doubled in size (2-4 hours).
Knead again briefly on a floured surface. Roll out to an 1/8 inch thickness. Cut into 3- to 4-inch circles.
Place filling in each circle. Follow the recipe for Mohn Filling.
Shape Hamantaschen by pinching edges together over the filling, leaving about half open. Fold over the flap and pinch edges firmly.
Arrange on a greased baking sheet, covering with a cloth. Let rise again in a warm place until doubled. Brush tops with egg yolk thinned with water.
Bake at 350°F for 15-20 minutes. Makes approximately 2 to 2 1/2 dozen cookies.
